skill.name: examples-code-reviewer
skill.description: AI-powered code review tool that analyzes code for bugs, style issues, and improvements
skill.summary: AI-powered code review tool that analyzes code for bugs, style issues, and improvements
skill.author: Enact Examples

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against the single SKILL.md file. The external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives caused by markdown backticks, schema descriptions, and example code output. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, filesystem access, or executable script behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Formatting
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md lines 89, 90, 91, 96, 101, and 104. These locations are markdown inline code labels and fenced example blocks, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are inside the output format section and example blocks. No command interpreter, script file, or tool invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at SKILL.md lines 5, 19, 22, 27, and 53. These lines describe code review inputs and a quality score, and they do not implement or recommend cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain natural language metadata and schema descriptions. There is no crypto API, hash function, cipher selection, or key handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported reconnaissance indicators at SKILL.md lines 98 and 111. These lines appear only in a small JavaScript review example and its suggested fix text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is confined to example source code and example review output. It does not collect host, user, network, or process information.

content.user_title: Review Code for Bugs, Style, Performance, and Security
content.value_statement: Manual code review can miss simple defects and inconsistent style issues. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce structured review findings with severity, line references, and fixes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews pasted source code for bugs, style issues, performance problems, and security risks.
Accepts an optional programming language hint when automatic detection is not enough.
Supports focused review modes for bugs, style, performance, security, or all areas.
Returns issue severity, likely line number, explanation, and suggested fix.
Provides a short quality summary and numeric score from 0 to 100.
limitations: It reviews only the code supplied in the prompt or tool input.
It does not run tests, compile code, or inspect a full repository by itself.
Line numbers may be approximate when input formatting is incomplete.
It cannot guarantee that every defect or vulnerability is found.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Review
use_cases.description: Review a small change before opening a pull request and fix obvious defects early.

use_cases.title: Security-Focused Review
use_cases.description: Check a function or handler for injection risks, unsafe operations, and missing validation.

use_cases.title: Learning-Oriented Feedback
use_cases.description: Get clear explanations for code issues and practical suggestions for improving implementation quality.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you want a general review of a short code snippet.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for bugs, style issues, performance concerns, and security risks. Return severity, line references, explanations, fixes, a summary, and a quality score.

prompt_templates.title: Bug-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when correctness is the main concern.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code with focus set to bugs. Look for logic errors, null handling problems, incorrect comparisons, boundary issues, and race conditions.

prompt_templates.title: Security Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when code handles user input, secrets, authentication, or data access.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code with focus set to security. Identify injection risks, unsafe operations, hardcoded secrets, missing validation, and authorization problems.

prompt_templates.title: Release Readiness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before merging a higher-risk change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a strict review of this code across bugs, style, performance, and security. Prioritize release-blocking issues and include concrete fixes for each finding.

output_examples.input: A JavaScript function uses assignment inside a find callback and then reads a property from the returned value.
output_examples.output: The review flags an error for the assignment operator, an error for missing null handling, and a warning for outdated variable declaration style.

output_examples.input: A web request handler builds a database query from raw user input.
output_examples.output: The review identifies a high-severity injection risk, explains the unsafe input flow, and recommends parameterized queries with validation.

output_examples.input: A loop repeatedly queries related records one item at a time.
output_examples.output: The review reports a performance concern, explains the N+1 query pattern, and suggests batching or eager loading.

best_practices: Provide the complete function or file section so line references and context are meaningful.
Set the focus field when you need a targeted review for bugs, style, performance, or security.
Verify suggested fixes with tests and project-specific standards before merging changes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the score as a substitute for tests, compilation, or human ownership.
Do not submit secrets, private credentials, or sensitive production data for review.
Do not ask for a broad repository audit when only a small snippet is supplied.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews supplied source code for bugs, style issues, performance concerns, and security risks.

faq.question: Does it execute the code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for read-only analysis and does not run, compile, or test code.

faq.question: Can I focus on one type of issue?
faq.answer: Yes. You can focus the review on bugs, style, performance, security, or all areas.

faq.question: What output should I expect?
faq.answer: Expect structured findings with severity, line references when possible, clear messages, suggested fixes, a summary, and a score.

faq.question: Is this useful for pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It works well for reviewing small changes before or during a pull request.

faq.question: Can it replace a human reviewer?
faq.answer: No. It helps find issues, but final decisions should use tests, project context, and human review.
